symptoms.symptom_name: Thick, adherent honey/golden-coloured crusts on an erythematous base

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Honey-coloured crusting is the classic hallmark of non-bullous impetigo.
HSV typically produces serous crusting over grouped vesicles, not thick golden crusts.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid contiguous spread across the face over 24-48 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autoinoculation and high contagiousness drive rapid contiguous spread.
A rapidly spreading facial vesicular-crusted rash is also the presentation of eczema herpeticum — the reason in-person exclusion is required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Onset after nasal trauma (nose-blowing) during a viral URTI — portal of entry

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A skin barrier breach with anterior-nares staphylococcal carriage is a classic seed for impetigo.
Impetigo is characteristically non-tender; pain would raise cellulitis or HSV.
Significant pain/tenderness and deep erythema would suggest cellulitis rather than superficial impetigo.

symptoms.symptom_name: No systemic toxicity — afebrile, feels well

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever and systemic upset supports a localised superficial infection and a lower triage tier.

symptoms.symptom_name: Shared towels/linen and close household contacts — transmission risk

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Not diagnostic, but central to the infection-control management plan given high contagiousness.
Not diagnostic, but required before recommending a beta-lactam antibiotic.
Regional lymphadenopathy reflects a primary bacterial process; sealed clinician palpation finding, not available over chat.
Tender regional nodes argue against a purely allergic dermatitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear oral mucosa — no intraoral ulceration

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mucosal sparing lowers the probability of primary herpetic gingivostomatitis / disseminated HSV, though it does not exclude eczema herpeticum.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bacterial swab culture isolating Staphylococcus aureus and/or Streptococcus pyogenes

loinc_ref.display: Bacteria identified in Wound by Culture

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Culture confirms the organism and sensitivities, but empiric treatment must not wait for it; never available in real time over chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: No grouped monomorphic punched-out vesicles or erosions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of the characteristic monomorphic punched-out herpetic lesions lowers the probability of eczema herpeticum — but confident assessment needs a clinician's inspection.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Honey-crusted, painless, rapidly spreading superficial facial lesions arising after a nasal skin breach is the recognisable pattern of non-bullous impetigo.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bacterial swab of a de-crusted lesion for microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms Staphylococcus aureus / Streptococcus pyogenes and guides therapy if the infection fails to respond; empiric treatment should start without waiting for the result.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Viral swab / PCR for herpes simplex if lesion morphology is atypical or eczema herpeticum is suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Distinguishes impetigo from herpetic disease when the diagnosis is uncertain on inspection — a must-not-miss branch.
Not indicated in a well, afebrile patient with localised superficial impetigo; reserve for systemic features or suspected deeper infection.
